name: SSE Event Bus & File Watching
description: Real-time event infrastructure providing in-process pub/sub event bus, filesystem watching with diff synthesis, SSE streaming endpoint, and event persistence. Watches OpenClaw and PACT directories, multiplexes typed events (agent actions, PACT transitions, task changes, errors), and streams to clients with filtering support. HMR-safe singleton pattern with graceful shutdown.

types.name: EventId
types.description: Branded string identifier for events (nanoid or UUID)

types.name: ConnectionId
types.description: Branded string identifier for SSE connections

types.name: EventVersion
types.description: String literal 'v1' for event schema version

types.name: PactEvent
types.description: PACT state transition and validation events

fields.name: watched_paths
fields.description: List of watched paths for watcher_ready events

types.name: Event
types.description: Discriminated union of all event types

types.name: EventPredicate
types.description: Function type (event: Event) => boolean for filtering

types.name: UnsubscribeFn
types.description: Function type () => void to unsubscribe from event bus

fields.name: awaitWriteFinish
fields.description: Options for debouncing writes {stabilityThreshold: number, pollInterval: number}

types.name: SSEFilterParams

validators.error_message: Type must be lowercase alphanumeric with optional dot-separated suffix

fields.name: lastEventId
fields.description: Resume from last received event ID

types.description: Query parameters for SSE endpoint filtering

types.name: PaginationParams
types.description: Pagination parameters for event history queries

types.name: EventFilters
types.description: Filter criteria for event history queries

fields.name: EVENT_RETENTION_DAYS
fields.description: Days to retain events in database

fields.name: IGNORED_PATTERNS
fields.description: Glob patterns to ignore when watching

types.name: EventBusError
types.description: Base error for event bus operations

types.name: WatcherError

fields.name: filePath
fields.description: Path to file that caused error

types.description: File watcher error with file context

types.name: ParseError

fields.name: filePath
fields.description: Path to file that failed to parse

types.description: File parsing error with location context

types.name: SerializationError

fields.name: path
fields.description: JSON path to field that failed serialization

types.description: Event serialization error with path to problematic field

types.name: MockEventBus
types.description: Mock event bus interface for testing

types.name: MockFileWatcher

fields.name: triggerChange
fields.description: Test helper to simulate file changes

types.description: Mock file watcher interface for testing

functions.name: createEventBus
functions.description: Factory function to create a new EventBus instance. Returns an event bus with active state, empty subscription registry, and ready for emit/subscribe operations.
postconditions: Returned bus has state='active'
Subscription registry is empty
Bus is ready to accept emit/subscribe calls

structured_side_effects.description: Creates new in-memory event bus with subscription registry

functions.name: emit
functions.description: Publish an event to the event bus. Synchronously notifies all matching subscribers based on their predicate filters. Does not throw on handler errors (logs instead). Non-blocking for persistence (fire-and-forget).

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: Event to publish (must have valid id, timestamp, type, version)

error_data.message: Cannot emit event: bus is destroyed

preconditions: Event must pass Zod schema validation
Event.id must be unique (generated via nanoid/uuid)
postconditions: All matching subscribers have been invoked
Event persistence attempted (async, non-blocking)
Handler errors logged but do not propagate

structured_side_effects.description: Invokes all registered handlers matching event predicate
Asynchronously persists event to database (non-blocking)
Logs handler errors and persistence failures

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of subscribers

functions.name: subscribe
functions.description: Register an event handler with optional predicate filter. Returns unsubscribe function. Handler is invoked synchronously when matching events are emitted. Predicate defaults to (event) => true (match all).

inputs.name: predicate
inputs.description: Filter function to select events (defaults to match all)

postconditions: Subscription registered with unique SubscriptionId
Handler will be invoked for future matching events
Returned unsubscribe function removes this subscription when called

structured_side_effects.description: Adds new subscription entry

functions.name: unsubscribe
functions.description: Remove a subscription by ID. Idempotent (safe to call multiple times). No-op if subscription ID not found.
postconditions: Subscription removed from registry (if existed)
Handler will no longer be invoked for future events

structured_side_effects.description: Removes subscription entry if present

functions.name: destroyEventBus
functions.description: Gracefully shutdown event bus. Unsubscribes all handlers, waits for in-flight handler executions (respecting AbortSignal), emits system.shutdown event, sets state to 'destroyed'. Idempotent.
postconditions: State set to 'destroyed'
All subscriptions cleared
system.shutdown event emitted before clearing subscriptions
No future emit/subscribe operations allowed

structured_side_effects.description: Clears subscription registry and updates state to 'destroyed'

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of active subscriptions

functions.name: getFileWatcher
functions.description: Singleton factory for FileWatcher. First call creates watcher with chokidar, subsequent calls return existing instance. Uses globalThis pattern for HMR safety in dev mode. Returns watcher in 'initializing' or 'ready' state.
postconditions: If first call: new FileWatcher created and stored in globalThis
If subsequent call: existing FileWatcher returned
Returned watcher state is 'initializing', 'ready', or 'error'
Only one watcher instance exists per process

structured_side_effects.description: Creates and stores FileWatcher instance on first call
Logs watcher creation and state transitions

functions.name: watch
functions.description: Start watching filesystem paths with chokidar. Synthesizes events from file changes (add/change/unlink), emits parse_error on malformed files, watcher_ready when initialization completes. Debounces file changes per DEBOUNCE_MS config. Idempotent (calling multiple times with same paths is safe).

inputs.name: paths
inputs.description: List of file/directory paths or glob patterns to watch

inputs.name: options
inputs.description: Chokidar watch options (ignored patterns, debounce settings)

error_cases.name: InvalidPath
error_cases.condition: Paths list is empty or contains invalid paths

error_cases.name: ChokidarInitFailed
error_cases.condition: Chokidar fails to initialize (permissions, missing dirs)

preconditions: Paths must be valid file system paths or globs
postconditions: Watcher state transitions to 'ready' on successful initialization
system.watcher_ready event emitted with watched_paths in payload
File change events debounced per DEBOUNCE_MS
Parse errors result in system.parse_error events (non-fatal)

structured_side_effects.description: Monitors files for changes using chokidar
Updates watcher state, emits events to bus on file changes
Logs watch initialization, errors, and file change events

functions.name: closeFileWatcher
functions.description: Shutdown file watcher and release chokidar resources. Stops all filesystem watching, emits no further events, sets state to 'closed'. Idempotent (safe to call multiple times).
postconditions: Watcher state set to 'closed'
Chokidar instance closed and resources released
No further file change events emitted
Singleton can be re-initialized via getFileWatcher() after close

structured_side_effects.description: Closes chokidar watchers and updates state to 'closed'
Logs watcher closure

functions.name: handleSSERequest
functions.description: Next.js Route Handler for GET /api/events. Validates query params with Zod, creates SSE stream (text/event-stream), subscribes to event bus with filters, sends events in SSE format (id:/event:/data:/retry:), emits heartbeat per HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS, handles client disconnect cleanup, supports Last-Event-ID header for reconnection.

inputs.name: request
inputs.description: Next.js Request object with query params

error_cases.name: InvalidQueryParams
error_cases.condition: Query params fail Zod SSEFilterParams validation

error_cases.name: EventBusUnavailable
error_cases.condition: Event bus is not active or destroyed

preconditions: Event bus is active and ready
Query params validate against SSEFilterParams schema
postconditions: Returns Response with headers: Content-Type: text/event-stream, Cache-Control: no-cache, Connection: keep-alive
system.connection_opened event emitted with new ConnectionId
Client receives filtered events in SSE format
Heartbeat events sent every HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS
Subscription cleaned up on client disconnect
If lastEventId provided: resume from that event (fetch from persistence layer)

structured_side_effects.description: Streams events over HTTP as text/event-stream
Subscribes to event bus during connection lifecycle
Reads historical events if lastEventId provided
Logs connection open/close and errors

functions.name: persistEvent
functions.description: Asynchronously persist event to SQLite activity_events table. Called non-blocking from emit(). Uses Promise.allSettled pattern to avoid blocking event propagation. Logs errors but does not throw.

error_cases.name: SerializationFailed
error_cases.condition: Event payload cannot be serialized to JSON (circular refs, BigInt without toJSON)

error_cases.name: DatabaseWriteFailed
error_cases.condition: SQLite insert fails (disk full, constraint violation)

preconditions: Event must be valid (already validated in emit)
postconditions: Event inserted into activity_events table (id, timestamp, type, payload JSON)
Old events beyond EVENT_RETENTION_DAYS pruned (async cleanup)
Errors logged but do not propagate to caller

structured_side_effects.description: Inserts event record and prunes old events
Logs serialization or database errors

functions.name: getEvents
functions.description: Query event history from SQLite activity_events table with filters and pagination. Returns events matching type/project/agent/time filters, ordered by timestamp, limited per pagination params. Used for /api/events/history and SSE reconnection (lastEventId).

inputs.name: filters
inputs.description: Filter criteria (type prefix, project/agent IDs, time range)

inputs.name: pagination
inputs.description: Limit, offset, order (defaults: limit=100, offset=0, order=desc)

error_cases.name: DeserializationFailed
error_cases.condition: Stored JSON payload cannot be parsed back to Event

error_data.message: Failed to deserialize event from database

postconditions: Returns list of Event objects matching filters
Events ordered by timestamp per pagination.order
Result limited per pagination.limit
Empty list if no matches

structured_side_effects.description: Queries events with WHERE clauses and LIMIT/OFFSET

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is result set size (bounded by limit)

functions.name: serializeEvent
functions.description: Convert Event object to SSE-compatible JSON string. Handles special types: BigInt (via toJSON or string conversion), Date (ISO 8601), Error (message + stack). Detects circular references and throws SerializationError with path to problematic field.

error_cases.name: UnsupportedType
error_cases.condition: Event payload contains non-serializable type (Symbol, Function)

preconditions: Event is a valid Event object (validated by Zod)
postconditions: Returns valid JSON string
BigInt fields converted to string or toJSON result
Date fields converted to ISO 8601 strings
Error fields converted to {message, stack} objects

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is payload size

functions.name: parseEventId
functions.description: Parse Last-Event-ID header value into EventId. Validates format (nanoid or UUID), returns null if invalid. Used for SSE reconnection to resume from last received event.

inputs.name: lastEventId
inputs.description: Last-Event-ID header value from SSE client

postconditions: Returns EventId if valid format
Returns null if invalid or empty string

functions.name: createEventPredicate
functions.description: Factory to create EventPredicate filter functions from SSEFilterParams. Builds predicate that checks event.type prefix, payload.project_id, payload.agent_id. Used by SSE endpoint to convert query params to subscription filter.

inputs.name: filterParams
inputs.description: Validated filter parameters from query string

postconditions: Returns function (event: Event) => boolean
Returned predicate checks event.type starts with filterParams.type (if provided)
Returned predicate checks payload.project_id === filterParams.project (if provided)
Returned predicate checks payload.agent_id === filterParams.agent (if provided)
All filters are AND-combined

functions.name: testSSEConnection
functions.description: Integration test helper that verifies SSE endpoint connectivity. Creates EventSource client, subscribes to /api/events, waits for system.connection_opened event, then disconnects. Returns true if connection successful, false otherwise. Used for health checks (AC6).

inputs.name: baseUrl
inputs.description: Base URL of Next.js app (e.g., http://localhost:3000)

error_cases.name: ConnectionTimeout
error_cases.condition: No connection_opened event received within timeoutMs

preconditions: Next.js app is running and serving /api/events
postconditions: Returns true if connection_opened event received within timeout
Returns false if timeout or connection error
EventSource connection closed after test

structured_side_effects.description: Creates SSE client connection for testing
Logs connection test results

invariants: Event IDs are unique within the system (enforced by nanoid/uuid)
All events have version='v1' (schema versioning for future evolution)
Event bus state transitions: active -> destroying -> destroyed (one-way)
FileWatcher state transitions: initializing -> ready|error, any -> closed
Only one FileWatcher instance exists per process (singleton via globalThis)
Event timestamps are strictly increasing within a single emit sequence
Subscription handlers never block emit() (async handlers run in background)
SSE connections emit exactly one connection_opened event per lifecycle
Persisted events older than EVENT_RETENTION_DAYS are automatically pruned
Event bus emit() never throws (errors logged, persistence non-blocking)
File watcher errors emit system.watcher_error events (never crash process)
Parse errors emit system.parse_error events with file path context
SSE heartbeat ensures connection liveness within 2*HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS
All Event payloads are JSON-serializable (no circular refs, Functions, Symbols)
EventPredicate filters are pure functions (no side effects, deterministic)
requires: chokidar ^3.x for filesystem watching with debouncing
zod for runtime schema validation (Event types, SSEFilterParams, etc.)
nanoid or uuid for generating EventId and ConnectionId
Next.js Route Handler types (Request, Response) for /api/events endpoint
Node.js fs/promises for file reading in parse error paths
AbortSignal for graceful shutdown timeout control
EventSource polyfill for testSSEConnection in Node.js environment
